title: Colombia vs Uzbekistan Predictions, Picks & Odds — Wednesday, June 17 World Cup Match
original_text:
Colombia vs Uzbekistan Predictions, Picks & Odds — Wednesday, June 17 World Cup Match
22+ years betting experience
Updated: Jun 16, 2026 , 12:00 PM ET • 4 min read
Colombia packs a mighty punch offensively and our expert Tom Oldfield believes this game has goals written all over it.
Jump to section:
Colombia can sometimes get lost in the shuffle among the giants of South America, but they’re well placed to make some noise in Group K, starting with Wednesday’s matchup against Uzbekistan.
Uzbek boss Fabio Cannavaro knows a thing or two about winning the World Cup, but my Colombia vs Uzbekistan predictions expect Luis Diaz and Co. to live up to the favorites tag in this opening fixture.
Take a closer look at this June 17 battle with my free World Cup picks.
Who will win Colombia vs Uzbekistan: Colombia -1.5 (+114)
COL @ UZB Match starts: 18 hrs
SPREAD
COL -1.5 (+114)
Trade now
All 3 COL vs UZB Picks
Colombia brushed off the doubters during a rollercoaster qualifying campaign, and I’m picking Los Cafeteros to be equally dismissive against Uzbekistan on Wednesday. After seven wins from their last 10 games, the Colombians arrive at this tournament with a real swagger, and their attacking options will cause nightmares for the Uzbek defense here.
It starts with James Rodriguez’s midfield guile — seven assists in qualifying — and Luiz Diaz’s direct style on the left. In-form striker Luis Suarez, who piled up 28 goals for Sporting this season, should be the primary beneficiary.
As well as Abdulkodir Khusanov, who played for Manchester City in the second half of the Premier League season, he carries a huge burden at the center of the Uzbekistan defense, and if the underdogs’ 3-4-1-2 shape descends into a deep-lying 5-4-1 formation, Colombia’s pressure will eventually pay off.
The Uzbeks lost both of their World Cup warm-up outings, and this opening Group K clash feels like a major step up in class against a physical Colombian squad that know what it takes to reach the knockout rounds, as they did in 2018.
This pick is playable up to +100.

All 3 COL vs UZB Picks
Buckle up for scoring chances in midweek, because Colombia don’t serve up many dull contests. This Over has been a winning ticket in eight of their past 10 games, and I’m banking on a free-scoring opener here.
After all, Los Cafeteros wrapped up their qualifying slate with 10 goals in the final three contests, headlined by a 6-3 thriller against Venezuela.
Expect chances at both ends with this attacking Colombian lineup, where fullbacks Daniel Munoz and Johan Mojica are encouraged to take up advanced positions, and their talented front four hold the key to a big goal haul at Azteca Stadium.
An early Colombia strike would force Uzbekistan out of their shell, but the Over is still appealing if this game is a slow burner. Nestor Lorenzo can turn to Jhon Cordoba and Cucho Hernandez off the bench in the search for goals, and his team has an aerial threat at free-kicks and corners.
While defensive solidity is the Uzbeks’ strength, it’s not as if their recent record is stacked with stalemates. They tied 2-2 with China in January, and Eldor Shomurodov is a man to watch on their raids forward.
Colombia vs Uzbekistan projected lineup
Colombia (4-2-3-1): Vargas; Munoz, Sanchez, Lucumi, Mojica; Lerma, Rios; , Jhon Arias, Rodriguez, Diaz; Suarez
Uzbekistan (4-2-3-1): Nematov; Alijonov, Khusanov, Ashurmatov, Karimov; Shukurov, Hamrobekov; Khamdamov, Fayzullaev, Urunov; Shomurodov
Colombia vs Uzbekistan injuries
Colombia: No injuries to report.
Uzbekistan: No injuries to report.
Colombia vs Uzbekistan weather
The forecast calls for a cloudy night at Azteca Stadium – after rain during the day – and temperatures in the low 60s by kick-off.
